summary: Backend testing: template-based news generation and Mercedes 'stay' persistence 1956-2025. All checks passed.
passed: POST /api/simulate for 1988 creates sim without running any race
POST /api/simulations/{id}/next twice: each race has news with title/subtitle/body/tag fields populated
News is template-based (no Gemini/LiteLLM calls observed in backend logs during test window)
Round-trip latency for /next is ~103-112ms (network incl.), backend processing well under 100ms
Multiple seeds (11,22,33,44,55,66) produce visibly different templates: DRAMA/DOMINA/TRIUNFO/CRAVA POLE variants
Reality created; current_year jumped to 1955 via pymongo; resolve lemans_1955 stay adds 11 team_override effects
Simulate 1980 (reality_id): Mercedes has Niki Lauda (per override 1979-1985)
Simulate 2000 (reality_id): Mercedes has Hakkinen + Coulthard (both overrides active 1996-2001/2005)
Simulate 2020 (reality_id): Mercedes has Hamilton + Bottas (Hamilton override 2007-2025, Bottas from base)
Team capacity max 2 preserved across 1980/2000/2020 - no team exceeds 2 drivers
notes: No Gemini calls after test start time (15:43+). Mercedes team_override chain works end-to-end 1956-2025 with Fangio/Moss/Brabham/Stewart/Peterson/Lauda/Prost/Hakkinen/Coulthard/Schumacher/Hamilton coverage.
